---
title: "Daphnia abundance analysis"
---

## Introduction

This document analyses Daphnia abundance under different treatments over time.

### Load libraries

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotrix)  
library(lme4)
library(car)
library(readr)
library(ggpubr)
library(readxl)
library(tidyr)
```

### Data import

```{r data-import, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df <- read.table("data_count_daphnia.txt", sep = "\t", header = TRUE)
```

### Data cleaning

```{r data-cleaning, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
df_daph <- df %>%
  rename(daph_count = `number_daphnia`) %>%
  mutate(
    timepoint = as.numeric(timepoint),
    daph_count = as.numeric(daph_count) / 9,
    treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("control", "heated"))
  )
```

### Summary statistics

```{r summary-statistics, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
daph_sum_trt <- df_daph %>%
  filter(!is.na(timepoint), treatment %in% c("control", "heated")) %>%
  group_by(timepoint, treatment) %>%
  summarise(
    n      = sum(!is.na(daph_count)),
    daph_av = mean(daph_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    daph_se = plotrix::std.error(daph_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

daph_ref0 <- df_daph %>%
  filter(timepoint == 0) %>%
  summarise(
    timepoint = 0,
    treatment = factor("ref", levels = c("ref", "control", "heated")),
    n       = sum(!is.na(daph_count)),
    daph_av = mean(daph_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    daph_se = plotrix::std.error(daph_count, na.rm = TRUE)
  )
```

### Prepare plot data

```{r prepare-plot-data, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
ref_for_lines <- daph_ref0 %>%
  select(timepoint, daph_av, daph_se) %>%
  crossing(
    treatment = factor(c("control", "heated"), levels = c("ref", "control", "heated"))
  )

line_df <- bind_rows(
  ref_for_lines,
  daph_sum_trt %>% mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("ref","control","heated")))
)

point_df <- bind_rows(
  daph_ref0,
  daph_sum_trt %>% mutate(treatment = factor(treatment, levels = c("ref","control","heated")))
)

time_levels <- sort(unique(point_df$timepoint))
line_df  <- line_df  %>% mutate(time_f = factor(timepoint, levels = time_levels))
point_df <- point_df %>% mutate(time_f = factor(timepoint, levels = time_levels))
```

### Daphnia abundance plot

```{r daphnia-plot, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, fig.width=6.3, fig.height=4.7}
px_daphnia <- ggplot() +
  geom_line(
    data = line_df,
    aes(x = time_f, y = daph_av, group = treatment, color = treatment),
    linewidth = 1,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  # error bars
  geom_errorbar(
    data = point_df,
    aes(x = time_f, y = daph_av, ymin = daph_av - daph_se, ymax = daph_av + daph_se, color = treatment),
    width = 0, linewidth = 0.8,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  # points
  geom_point(
    data = point_df,
    aes(x = time_f, y = daph_av, color = treatment),
    size = 3,
    na.rm = TRUE
  ) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = c("ref" = "#4D4D4D", "control" = "#0072B2", "heated" = "#D55E00")
  ) +
  labs(
    x = "Time (weeks)",
    y = "Daphnia abundance (count L⁻¹)"
  ) +
  theme_classic(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    panel.border = element_blank(),
    axis.line = element_line(color = "black"),
    axis.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text  = element_text(size = 14),
    legend.position = "none",
    legend.title = element_blank(),
    legend.text  = element_text(size = 14)
  )

px_daphnia
```

### Statistical analysis

```{r statistical-analysis, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
m_daph <- lmer(daph_count ~ treatment * timepoint + (1|pond) + (1|block), data = df_daph %>% filter(timepoint != 0))
Anova(m_daph, test = "F")
summary(m_daph)
```
